video_summary: This video comprehensively introduces CI/CD (Continuous Integration Continuous Delivery), explaining its core concepts, necessity by contrasting it with manual development problems, detailing stages like build, test, and deployment, distinguishing between continuous delivery and continuous deployment, outlining popular CI/CD tools, and describing various deployment strategies.

recommended_sequence.description: A set of practices that combines software development (Dev) and IT operations (Ops), of which CI/CD is a core component.
recommended_sequence.reason: 'DevOps' is a root concept (no prerequisites) and must be understood first because it directly enables: Continuous Integration Continuous Delivery (CI/CD).

recommended_sequence.description: The entire process that changes in any application go through, from development to deployment, involving steps like coding, building, testing, and releasing.
recommended_sequence.reason: 'Software Development Lifecycle (SDLC)' is a root concept (no prerequisites) and must be understood first because it directly enables: Manual Software Development Process (Pre-CI/CD Problems).

recommended_sequence.description: The initial stage of the SDLC where new functionalities or improvements are designed and coded by developers.
recommended_sequence.reason: 'Feature Development' is a root concept (no prerequisites) and must be understood first because it directly enables: Build Stage.

recommended_sequence.description: A system (like Git) used to manage changes to source code over time, allowing multiple developers to work concurrently, manage different code branches, and merge their contributions.
recommended_sequence.reason: 'Version Control System (VCS) / Git' is a root concept (no prerequisites) and must be understood first because it directly enables: Build Stage, Integration Hell.

recommended_sequence.description: A human-friendly data serialization standard, commonly used for configuration files and defining instructions for CI/CD pipelines.
recommended_sequence.reason: 'YAML (Yet Another Markup Language)' is a root concept (no prerequisites) and must be understood first because it directly enables: CI/CD Pipeline.

recommended_sequence.description: A component that distributes incoming network traffic across multiple servers or instances, preventing overload on any single server and improving overall application availability and responsiveness.
recommended_sequence.reason: 'Load Balancer' is a root concept (no prerequisites) and must be understood first because it directly enables: Rolling Deployment.

recommended_sequence.description: An automated approach to software development that involves continuously integrating code changes, building and testing them, and then preparing them for release or deployment. It is the automation of the software development lifecycle steps.
recommended_sequence.reason: 'Continuous Integration Continuous Delivery (CI/CD)' builds upon DevOps (importance 10/10) and is required before studying: Continuous Integration (CI), Continuous Delivery (CD), CI/CD Pipeline, Deployment Strategies, Docker and Kubernetes.

recommended_sequence.description: The traditional software development approach where build, test, and deployment steps were performed manually, leading to human errors, time consumption, and inefficiencies.
recommended_sequence.reason: 'Manual Software Development Process (Pre-CI/CD Problems)' builds upon Software Development Lifecycle (SDLC) (importance 7/10) and is required before studying: Integration Hell, Infrequent Releases.

recommended_sequence.description: The process of compiling source code, along with its dependencies and libraries, into deployable artifacts such as binary files, executable files, or packages (e.g., .apk for Android).
recommended_sequence.reason: 'Build Stage' builds upon Feature Development, Version Control System (VCS) / Git (importance 8/10) and is required before studying: Software Testing, Continuous Integration (CI).

recommended_sequence.description: Containerization (Docker) and container orchestration (Kubernetes) tools that are commonly integrated with CI/CD workflows to enhance portability, scalability, and management of applications, especially in large organizations.
recommended_sequence.reason: 'Docker and Kubernetes' builds upon Continuous Integration Continuous Delivery (CI/CD) (importance 3/10) and is a terminal concept in this learning path.

recommended_sequence.description: A problem arising from manual integration processes, where merging code from multiple developers at once leads to frequent conflicts and errors, making the integration process extremely difficult and time-consuming.
recommended_sequence.reason: 'Integration Hell' builds upon Manual Software Development Process (Pre-CI/CD Problems), Version Control System (VCS) / Git (importance 6/10) and is a terminal concept in this learning path.

recommended_sequence.description: The process of verifying an application's functionality and quality, including unit tests (by developers), integration tests (component interaction), and regression tests (new changes don't break old features).
recommended_sequence.reason: 'Software Testing' builds upon Build Stage (importance 8/10) and is required before studying: Release, Continuous Integration (CI).

recommended_sequence.description: The phase in the SDLC where the application, after successful testing, is made available for use by end-users.
recommended_sequence.reason: 'Release' builds upon Software Testing (importance 6/10) and is required before studying: Deployment, Continuous Delivery (CD), Infrequent Releases.

recommended_sequence.description: The practice of automating the build and test stages of the software development lifecycle, triggered by every code change to quickly detect and address integration issues.
recommended_sequence.reason: 'Continuous Integration (CI)' builds upon Continuous Integration Continuous Delivery (CI/CD), Build Stage, Software Testing (importance 9/10) and is required before studying: Continuous Delivery (CD), CI/CD Pipeline.

recommended_sequence.description: The act of making an application, code, or software accessible within a specific environment. Common environments include development (dev), staging (pre-production), and production (prod) for end-users.
recommended_sequence.reason: 'Deployment' builds upon Release (importance 9/10) and is required before studying: Continuous Delivery (CD), Continuous Deployment, Infrequent Releases, Deployment Strategies.

recommended_sequence.description: The practice of automating the release and deployment stages, ensuring the application is always in a deployable state. It typically involves a manual approval step before deployment to production.
recommended_sequence.reason: 'Continuous Delivery (CD)' builds upon Continuous Integration Continuous Delivery (CI/CD), Continuous Integration (CI), Release, Deployment (importance 9/10) and is required before studying: Continuous Deployment, CI/CD Pipeline.

recommended_sequence.description: A consequence of manual software development processes where code freezes, time-consuming manual testing, and manual deployment lead to long intervals between new software releases for users.
recommended_sequence.reason: 'Infrequent Releases' builds upon Manual Software Development Process (Pre-CI/CD Problems), Release, Deployment (importance 6/10) and is a terminal concept in this learning path.

recommended_sequence.description: Methods used in CI/CD to deploy new versions of applications efficiently, aiming for minimal to zero downtime and providing mechanisms for quick rollback in case of issues.
recommended_sequence.reason: 'Deployment Strategies' builds upon Deployment, Continuous Integration Continuous Delivery (CI/CD) (importance 9/10) and is required before studying: Blue-Green Deployment, Canary Deployment, Rolling Deployment.

recommended_sequence.description: An advanced form of Continuous Delivery where applications are automatically deployed to the production environment after passing all automated tests, without any manual intervention or approval.
recommended_sequence.reason: 'Continuous Deployment' builds upon Continuous Delivery (CD), Deployment (importance 9/10) and is a terminal concept in this learning path.

recommended_sequence.description: An automated workflow, typically defined by scripts (e.g., YAML files), that orchestrates the stages of CI/CD, from code commit to deployment, ensuring a streamlined and continuous process.
recommended_sequence.reason: 'CI/CD Pipeline' builds upon Continuous Integration Continuous Delivery (CI/CD), Continuous Integration (CI), Continuous Delivery (CD), YAML (Yet Another Markup Language) (importance 8/10) and is required before studying: CI/CD Tools.

recommended_sequence.description: A deployment strategy using two identical, separate environments: 'Blue' for the current production version and 'Green' for the new version. All traffic is switched from Blue to Green; if problems arise, traffic can be instantly reverted to Blue.
recommended_sequence.reason: 'Blue-Green Deployment' builds upon Deployment Strategies (importance 9/10) and is a terminal concept in this learning path.

recommended_sequence.description: A deployment strategy where a new version is gradually rolled out to a small subset of users first. If stable, more users are progressively directed to the new version, allowing for real-world testing with minimal impact.
recommended_sequence.reason: 'Canary Deployment' builds upon Deployment Strategies (importance 9/10) and is a terminal concept in this learning path.

recommended_sequence.description: A deployment strategy where new versions are deployed incrementally to a subset of instances within an environment. A load balancer directs traffic, allowing for gradual updates and testing without taking the entire application offline.
recommended_sequence.reason: 'Rolling Deployment' builds upon Deployment Strategies, Load Balancer (importance 9/10) and is a terminal concept in this learning path.

recommended_sequence.description: Software platforms and applications that facilitate the implementation and management of CI/CD pipelines, such as GitHub Actions and Jenkins.
recommended_sequence.reason: 'CI/CD Tools' builds upon CI/CD Pipeline (importance 8/10) and is a terminal concept in this learning path.
